message:
feat(pager,core,wal): narrow WAL read path to shared lock, move PhaseHistogram to heap, isolate global metrics tests (bd-db300.3.8.7, bd-db300.3.8.1, bd-db300.3.8.3)

WAL write-lock narrowing (bd-db300.3.8.7):
- Add read_page_pinned(&self) and supports_pinned_reads() to WalBackend
  trait with default implementations so page reads can take a shared
  RwLock guard when the backend has a pinned snapshot
- Implement in WalBackendAdapter, resolving frames from pinned read
  snapshot without &mut self
- Update three pager read sites (read_page_for_txn variants, cached WAL
  fast path) to try shared-lock first with write-lock fallback
- Instrument five wake-reason counters (notify, timeout, flusher_takeover,
  failed_epoch, busy_retry) at group-commit condvar exit points

PhaseHistogram stack-to-heap migration (bd-db300.3.8.1, bd-db300.3.8.3):
- Replace [AtomicU64; 4096] inline array with Box<[AtomicU64]> to avoid
  32KB stack frames
- Migrate GLOBAL_CONSOLIDATION_METRICS from const static to LazyLock
- Add test-serialization mutex (GLOBAL_CONSOLIDATION_METRICS_TEST_LOCK)
  and RAII reset guard so histogram tests no longer interfere across
  threads
- Wrap every commit_path_histograms test in with_global_metrics() and
  change assertions from >= (flaky) to == (exact, now starting from zero)
- Add ResetTelemetryGlobals RAII guard for telemetry composite test
- Gate commit_path_histograms module behind #[cfg(test)]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
